title: release: add bounded notes and markdown forecast
summary: Solid, well-scoped PR. The `until` CLI flag is a clean pass-through to a pre-existing `GenerateOptions.until` field. The `forecast-markdown` renderer is correctly structured, well-tested, and integrates with existing model types (`ForecastRelease.currentVersionDisplay`, `CommitDisplay`, `PublishRecord`) without any mismatches. One minor structural inconsistency in the markdown output: the Primary and Cascades sections use `###` headings, but the publish metadata is rendered as bare bullet items under the summary line with no section heading, breaking the document hierarchy.

findings.title: Publish metadata section lacks a `###` heading, breaking document hierarchy
findings.body:
The `### Primary (N)` and `### Cascades (N)` sections both have headings, but the publish state/history block is emitted as plain bullet list items directly under the summary line with no heading. If a reader pastes this into an issue or PR body, the publish section is visually unanchored and inconsistent with the rest of the document structure.

Consider adding a heading like `### Publish` or `### Publish Metadata` before the conditional block:
```typescript
if (publishState !== 'idle' || publishHistory.length > 0) {
  output``
  output`### Publish`
  output``
  output`- Publish state: \`${publishState}\``
  ...
}
```

residual_risks: The `--until` boundary is not validated against known tags/SHAs at the CLI layer — an invalid ref will only fail at the git-log level, potentially producing an unhelpful error message rather than a clear user-facing diagnostic. This matches the existing `--since` behavior, so it is a pre-existing limitation rather than a regression introduced here.
The nested ternary for format dispatch in `forecast.ts` is now 3 levels deep; adding a fourth format will require a refactor toward a dispatch table to stay readable.
